reviews.title: works great for fungus gnats
reviews.text:
works great for fungus gnats in house plants; and I am confident to use it on food (ie tomatoes and vegetables started indoors)

Update 2021: I've been using this for years now with great results against fungus gnats in houseplants and garden plants started indoors. I avoid cheap bagged soils (Miracle Grow, Kellogs, etc.) that are usually infested with gnat eggs. Instead, I mix peat moss, vermiculite, and mature compost and I mix some of these "mosquito bits" into the soil before filling the containers (pots or plant bags). When my wife waters, she can sprinkle bits in the watering can and let sit overnight. Using that water and the treated soil, gnats are eradicated. I have as much as a yard of soil in my house - enough plants to fill a large room wall to wall -- and no gnats. This stuff works. I recommend buying it by the 5-gallon bucket if you use it outdoors or have many houseplants.

These are extremely comfortable, with one caveat: I wish they were a little softer. I can wear them all day with good cushioned socks without fatigue, but if I wear moisture-wicking athletic socks (no cushioning), my feet start to get sore after a while, due to the firm nature of the foam these are made of. I know it is necessary for the support, but please Superfeet, put a soft, cushioned layer (like RoadRunner's inserts) on TOP of the foam. Then they would be absolutely perfect. Still the best insole out there for support though. 4.75 stars.

reviews.title: Don't buy from Amazon. Get from a real store
reviews.text:
I agree with other reviewers that this is crap.

The green plastic part is firm, but the foam part is so painful. Basically no sole, so trying to live a day in these, is insane. I'd rather be without shoes than use these insoles.

I tried these for several months before I tossed them.

It felt like walking on cheap plastic and a cardboard box...

Save your money. Get the real product. It's probably better.

Wish I could return these!!!

reviews.title: Excellent replacement Insole
reviews.text: I have had six pairs of these in various shoes over the years, hiking boots, tennis shoes, and work shoes. I have never had a bad pair. Excellent heel cushioning effect. Break in quickly. They feel a little tight on my arch at first due to the fact that I have a fairly flat arch, but within a day of wearing I no longer notice anything. These work great to give a old pair of shoes new life. I still flinch at paying the high cost but always glad I spent the money after walking for a day.

reviews.title: Not great downhill though
reviews.text: It fit right into my mens size 9 shoes no problem. Works decent on flat ground and uphill walks. Not great downhill though. Then again, it may just be how my feet are. I'm a little overweight and was just looking for something to replace the regular insoles of my shoes. Honestly, I would've liked a little more cushion.

reviews.title: Quality of Life Improvement with Simple Inserts and Change of Socks
reviews.text: I went to Fleet Feet store and was told I have inflexible arches because they are very high. I was given the recommendation to purchase Superfeet GREEN Full Length Insole Size B, in addition to Balega socks, and life has been so much better ever since. Granted they were more expensive, but I have more energy throughout the day and I do not come home with sore feet after a day on the job. I work as a nurse and am on my feet for 12 hrs per day. I used to come home and get foot cramps every night with super sore, tired feet; however, this combination has been an amazing Quality of Life Improver!!!

reviews.title: Reduced my acne inflammation
reviews.text:
There is only .34 fluid ounces in this tiny bottle but I can get a lot of use out of it because only a couple of drops are needed. The "Gya labs" website states that all essential oils should be diluted with one tablespoon of carrier oil of one's choice to two drops of essential oil.

I used one tablespoon of jojoba oil and tried to use only two drops of this manuka oil but the bottle dispenses the drops quickly, so my concoction contained about five or six drops.

The smell of this oil is antiseptic to me; almost like tea tree oil. After shaking to mix the carrier with this oil, I put some on either end of a Q-Tip and dabbed it on my acne. The inflammation went down overnight.

Obviously this lives up to its claims of being an anti-inflammatory. It is for external use only and supposedly good for muscle and joint relief and as a skin aid for acne, rashes, bug bites, and mild wound infections.

As with every essential oil I get, I wish there was an instruction pamphlet with it on how to use. I am not that well-versed on essential oils and some of the books I read differ in their recommendations; therefore, it would be great if a vendor included a small insert. Nevertheless, this works for me. My only disappointment is that the oil dispenses too quickly from the dropper top and I find it impossible to just dispense two drops into my carrier oil.

reviews.text:
Sweet, flavorful. Works in coffee, smoothies, or adding to pancake mix (muffin mix, oatmeal, yogurt, tea). Smaller dose per packet compared to that taken from their canister. Nice if you don't need much at once, or want something for on the go use, travel.. This product is nice way to try out their flavored options before committing to a canister. They have available any of these flavors in larger canisters (and at 12 grams per serving, versus the 6 grams in the packets).

Collagen peptides can be dissolved in cold water, but some do best added to warm or room temp water before using it in cold applications. This can be true of most collagen peptides, but I find it especially so with flavored varieties. Instant dissolve in hot coffee.

Great Lakes is a brand I trust. Great Lakes brand is what started it all in hydrolyzed collagen from grass fed/pasture raised cows, which is collagen reduced down to collagen peptides which is easier on the gut, easier to absorb, and more effective. I started using Great Lakes well over 10 years ago. They had regular collagen (have to use with hot water, and if it cools you have jello, and their hydrolyzed, which can be used in cold or hot liquid, and never gels. In the forms of bovine or porcine. Is pig gelatin even still a thing?). Now collagen peptides (aka hydrolyzed) are easily available. Many good brands, most at similar price point. It is a competitively priced product.

Great Lakes has branched out into flavors. I tend to prefer plain hydrolyzed bovine collagen. The flavors tend to have added sweetness, which is ok but not for how I like to use it. I do find the flavored options nice for use in smoothies, and for adding to pancake or muffin mixes.

4 stars. Good collagen, good flavors, albeit much sweeter than I normally go for, little less collagen that I normally go for at once. The product description is describing a canister product not the stick product. Take closer look at the product pictures for more info.

reviews.title: quite sweet with both Stevia & Monk fruit. Better for baked goods & smoothies
reviews.text:
I am a long time user of Great Lakes collagen products. I have always experienced quality products from them. While this is still quality, the overall taste is not impressing me. I was curious about the Chai flavor as I often drink chai tea. I usually add collagen powder BUT for some reason I tend to taste even unflavored collagen powder in my tea (not so when I have coffee). I figured.,. ooh, chai flavored collagen powder should work well in my tea or coffee. And, it does.. the part that throws me off is how sweet this is. It has a combo of stevia and monk fruit extract. I think it could skip the stevia and the taste would be more mellow and lightly sweet rather than faint and overly sweet.

It does pair really well with my chai tea bags (save for the added sweetness), and it is fine for my coffee, but the chai flavor is lost a little amongst the overly sweet aspect of it. This is preference, so the extra sweet aspect may be perfect for you. It wont keep me from trying/buying other Great Lakes products. They are a brand I trust.. still.

I imagine--for me-- it would be particularly good in baked goods, pancake mix, waffle mix, smoothies..

reviews.title: excellent quality collagen hydrolysate
reviews.text:

And although I dont have total loyalty to the brand, I do find myself coming back around to it. It is just a quality product I trust.

Blends perfectly into cold or hot liquids, has no real discernible taste. Easy to use container- just pour or shake out what is needed. Love it.

reviews.title: subtle natural tasting vanilla, not too sweet
reviews.text:
If you are looking at this product then you likely already use similar products/ingredients and are following a keto diet. So I will comment strictly on taste, texture, overall perception of quality.

I am a big fan of Great Lakes Collagen, so I feel like I have a built in bias. But if it were bad, I would certainly say so. I have been using this brand for years!! I've been adding stuff to my coffee before it became a thing. There was a time when this was pretty much the only brand around for collagen hydrolsate/collagen peptides. Fortunately it was a quality product from the get go, and remains that way.

A vanilla collagen coffee booster that wasn't laden in sugar appealed to me. I am not at all keto, but I do "try" to watch my excess sugar. I often drink my first cup of coffee black, not eat anything for a few hours, then go and make another coffee (caffeinated, haff caff, or decaff depending on how I feel) then with this cup I usually turn it into a smoothie. I add my collagen in, and then sometimes other stuff (mushroom powder for example). Sometimes I use a plain collagen powder, sometimes I go for a vanilla flavored one. SO this style of product was already familiar to me. Then it being a Great Lakes product? I gotta know.

Ok, so I love how seamlessly it blends into my coffee. I usually just stir it in. I have no clumps. I never use anything but a spoon to stir it in. Very easy. It has a light, natural vanilla taste-- some are weird or off putting so I am so very thankful that this has a nice natural flavor. If you need a strong, artificial taste, then this may not be for you.

I like the addition of MCT oil powder. Unless I am using a blender, then I dont like just adding direct oil to my coffee (using a blender emsulifies oils and creams up nicely like a froth), otherwise makes for oily sipping.

The level of sweetness is spot on. Not too sweet. Just right. SO PERFECT for me. Sometimes I drink my coffee smoothies and cringe, but this is always good tasting--to me.

I usually use two heaping scoops. Serving size is 2 scoops for a 25 day supply. So for me more like 21.

The fact that I have been remembering to take this every day demonstrates (to me) that it is a worthwhile product and has much to do with my rating of 5. Some stuff I start, then stop. So I have hundreds of dollars of stuff just collecting dust or ending up in the trash. Or occasionally give away. This is just too easy and nice tasting to not continue with. I'll likely stick with this. Maybe I will check to see what other new stuff they have since come up with since the times when they offered just the "green can" or "red can" :)

reviews.title: quality product, I purchase over and over
reviews.text:
Not a lot of protein compared to some, but it is reasonable amount. I love how easily it blends and that it is one of the early collagen products sourced from pasture raised, grass fed cows. As a hydrolysate, it mixes easily with cold liquids. Many other products have come out that are of similar style and value, but I really like this brand and will continue to purchase it. [[VIDEOID:34e80b0509f213b75d2b1476c8258724]] Well, where to start. Let's start on a positive note. The quality of the materials seems to be very good, and the colors are great.

Now let's move to reality. I truly don't think I have the largest foot in the country. (Sasquatch could not be located for a comment, so I'm sticking with a big ol' no.) I wear a ladies 8.5 or a men's 6.5-7 (USA sizing). I have had plantar fasciitis and heel spurs, and my feet are fairly flat. Yikes. I could not get these on my feet. I even tried them both directions, just in case. Still a big nope.

Now, I have chubby legs, but again, not circus sideshow worthy. IF I had been able to get them on my leg, I cannot imagine how much the circulation would be cut off. The marks on my leg would be deep.
Ladies, think trying to put on a pair of pantyhose that is two or three sizes too small, and you are bloated. Yeah. Like that, only tighter.

I don't know if I got a bad pair or this is how these things fit. I see other photos that look normal. Others appeared to get them on their legs without issues. The pair I got would barely go over a summer sausage.

So, I cannot recommend them. Too tight, binding is ridiculously small. Just no way.

reviews.title: Rebuilds Enamel...Reverses Micro Cavities...Tastes Delicious!
reviews.text: Enamel is the hard outer surface layer on teeth that helps protect and prevent tooth decay. It is the hardest mineral substance in the body-even stronger than bone.  That's amazing! And oh so VERY necessary and important to the health of teeth.  I've heard my own dentist as well as dentists on TV comment that once enamel is gone, its gone.  Once that happens, cavities, sensitivity and even loss of teeth are very real possibilities.  I've experienced not only cavities+fillings, but also root canals (these are NOT fun at all...)+crowns and yes, tooth loss. I've lost 4 adult teeth over the years. Two due to an accident, two that broke too much to be repaired with root canal+crowns. Thankfully, I was able to get dental implants for all 4 missing teeth.  While I'm thankful and SO appreciative for my implants, they're not cheap at all.  My dental insurance paid next to nothing-nearly the entire cost for all 4 implants came out of pocket and boy, did that wreak havoc on our bank account. I don't want to do that again. And I REALLY don't want to have another root canal or even another filling. I'd never heard of Nano-hydroxyapatite  or nHa before. nHa is the mineral in tooth enamel-its what protects our teeth from decay and harm.  Modern science has now created a  paste made from nHa and that very paste is an active ingredient in BOKA Coco-Ginger toothpaste! Thanks to the nHa in BOKA, this paste will reduce tooth sensitivity, reverse micro cavities, and best of all..REBUILD ENAMEL!  And that is unbelievably incredible and amazing to me. It truly is! I must admit, I did not think I would care for BOKA Coco Ginger toothpaste at first. Per the instructions, a pea size amount of the paste is to be placed on the toothbrush and its not necessary to wet the toothbrush prior to use . I ALWAYS do that before brushing my teeth. (yes, i've known for years you're not supposed to wet it  before brushing with regular toothpaste as well but I always have) But I was determined to try BOKA so I followed the instructions and didn't wet the brush. The next thing I noticed and didnt' care for (at first) was that BOKA doesn't foam like regular toothpaste. I worried my teeth weren't getting clean enough.  But worst of all-there's no need to rinse the mouth after brushing with BOKA. THAT one really got to me.  Want to know something? I got over it quickly.  It stopped being an issue for me almost immediately. In fact, all of the 'issues' I had with BOKA at first vanished pretty much after I used it for the first and second time ever. I no longer cared that it didn't foam. I no longer cared about not wetting the brush or not rinsing my mouth after brushing. Because BOKA toothpaste is incredible.  I do NOT miss strong peppermint toothpaste that burned my mouth after use. I do not miss toothpaste foam dribbling down my chin while brushing.  Instead, I much prefer and enjoy the taste of BOKA-coconut and ginger.  Its really good and  not overwhelming at all. It does not burn my mouth like regular toothpaste did. I USED to have issues with sensitivity while brushing. I no longer do thanks to BOKA.  It works great on my teeth as well as my fillings, crowns and dental implants! Best of all-I'm not only reversing micro cavities, I'm doing what was once impossible-I'm rebuilding precious tooth enamel!  Simply brushing with BOKA twice a day for 2 minutes each time is repairing my teeth and making them stronger. And BOKA is free of parabens, artificial flavors/colors and fluoride. Its made in the USA as well! I love knowing BOKA is restoring my teeth and helping to keep them healthy. I love the clean, fresh feeling after brushing my teeth with BOKA as well.  BOKA is the only toothpaste I will use from now on. There's NO reason to use any other kind/brand. For all that BOKA does, the price is more than reasonable. I'm hoping it is soon part of Amazon's 'subscribe and save' plan.  If you want to keep your teeth as healthy as possible and help REVERSE damage, look no further than BOKA.

reviews.title: Unusual Toothpaste, But I Like It
reviews.text:
I am always looking for non sodium lauryl sulfate toothpastes to use. Some are nice some not so much. Let's take a look at this.

WHAT IS THIS LIKE?
This tube comes with a metal seal on the inside under the screw on cap. It is a nice pink colored cap so you can tell what it is at a glance.

TEXTURE
This is not a really thick toothpaste, but it is not too runny either.

FLAVOR
I LOVE the flavor of this. It is a great balance of flavor. Neither the coconut or ginger is too strong, so it is pleasant to use.

PERFORMANCE
They recommend that you use this for a couple of minutes and then spit it out but you don't have to rinse well as it will continue to work on your teeth if left on them. I tend to spit out the excess and rinse the center of my mouth avoiding the teeth. This is not a problem as it does not feel gaggy like most paste does to me.

DOES IT HELP?
I have had no issues of problems with this paste, but I have not seen any remarkable results either. I can say that it does not leave my teeth overly sensitive and I have not had any bad reactions.

THE BOTTOM LINE
For a different type of toothpaste that tastes good, and seems to perform well, this is a good choice.

MY RATING
FIVE STARS so far. I like this and would buy it again. Some pastes really make my teeth more sensitive. This does not.

reviews.title: Unique Toothpaste, Unusual Process, But Works
reviews.text:
When it comes to toothpaste, I am very particular about what we use. Since ridding our home of Sodium Lauryl Sulfate products, it is amazing the yearly problems that never came back. Let's take a look at this toothpaste.

WHAT IS THIS LIKE?
This has a list of ingredients in it which are a bit unusual for toothpaste and some that are typical. The consistency is what I would call a polishing consistency. It is soft and yet holds up to putting on a brush.

FLAVOR
The flavor is a gentle mint. It is not one of those mints that blow you over backwards when you put it in your mouth, but neither is it so weak you wonder if it is working. It does not have a fake taste to it, only a different type. The balance of flavors is interesting and it does leave breath feeling fresh.

HOW TO USE
This is different in that it suggests using it with a dry brush, brushing for two minutes. Then it encourages you NOT to spit it out but leave it on the teeth.

HOW DO I LIKE THE PROCESS?
I am not sure about leaving this in my mouth. First of all, I am not crazy about ingesting sorbitol. Secondly, it is instinctive to spit out toothpaste. I have tried only rinsing my tongue and leaving it on my teeth, and that seems like an okay compromise.

DOES IT WORK?
I have to say I have very sensitive teeth and this really does seem to reduce sensitivity after using it for almost a week and a half now. I use it once a day.

THE BOTTOM LINE
If you like the idea of a unique toothpaste that leans more natural and has a complex minty flavor that does not blow you over, this might be worth a try. It does seem to help with sensitivity but leaving it on the teeth feels a bit weird no matter how hard I try.

MY RATING
FOUR STARS. The sorbitol has me removing 1 star. I would rather have this without that if they want it to be left on my teeth. It does seem to help and work, so I still do like it.

reviews.title: Good quality compression socks; I ordered a couple of sizes larger
reviews.text:
Periodically I wear compression socks to help me with my plantar fasciitis. However, I find it incredibly difficult to get those socks on my feet and they tend to be uncomfortable over time. I ordered these in the 4XL because I wanted to make them much easier to get on. Note that I usually take a large so this size goes on easily, yet I still feel a light compression in the foot and arch area.

These socks are not only nice looking and in an attractive color but they are also comfortable. They are also quality socks.

I wash them in a lingerie bag in the washer and then lay them flat to dry or tumble dry on low.

In summary, these are well made compression socks and are of medium thickness. They offer the most compression in the foot and arch area where I need it. They also have compression in the calves but because I ordered too large a size, that compression is not noticeable in that area. The current price of $25.95 is competitively priced for these

reviews.title: Poor customer service, fragile material & poor/confusing and untimely care instructions
reviews.text:
The first time my hubby wore these he liked them. Then I washed them in the washing machine (I received no instructions with my socks that stated special care was necessary). The socks felt larger to my husband the second time he used them. Over a week later I received an email titled "Your recent order and washing instructions." The socks, I was instructed, had to be washed in cold water by hand. I replied and said that I wished they'd have told me so before I ruined them in the washer. They said not to worry that's how they always wash them too. I thought to myself, how could I not be worried/frustrated/mad that I had just ruined these expensive socks? ...I decided to let it go and planned on buying from another company next month...but today I received another email from them titled the same and the instructions first say to wash them with warm water...then further down to use just cold water...and then they go back to the warm water. They also reinforce the instructions to never wash them in the washer as this will ruin the socks.

Bottom line: poor customer service, poor setting of expectations, poor/confusing care instructions, lack of timely expectation setting. I would never waste my money on these again!
